title: ZChess
text: Home * Engines * ZChess ZChess , a WinBoard compatible chess engine by Franck Zibi , written in C++ , in April 2001 renamed to Pharaon . ZChess evolved from a C port of HpChess incorporating object oriented paradigms, and using a bitboard board representation [1] and in particular rotated bitboards to generate sliding piece attacks. ZChess applies PVS with iterative deepening and aspiration windows , using a transposition table with multiple probes, pawn- and king safety hash tables , SEE for move ordering and pruning , adaptive null move pruning with R = 2, 3, and heavily prunes not only in quiescence search . Move ordering considers hash-table move , winning captures by SEE, killer heuristic , counter move heuristic , and history heuristic [2] . Contents 1 Tournaments 2 Selected Games 2.1 FCCC 1998 2.2 WMCCC 2000 3 See also 4 Forum Posts 5 External Links 6 References Tournaments [ edit ] ZChess had its debut at the FCCC 1997 and became runner up behind Chess Wizard at the FCCC 1998 . It further participated at the WMCCC 2000 in London, the IPCCC 2001 and CCT2 online tournament, before it was renamed to Pharaon . Against humans, ZChess 1.2 played the 1999 Aubervilliers Tournament , scoring 9½/12 (one loss) with a TPR of 2300 Elo [3] .
